cross_references.detail: Steel frame so favors lightweight metal roofing/cladding (Ajiya Metal). RC frame so supports heavier clay/concrete tile roofing (PPCH). Steel truss selection so triggers roof covering specification in Agent 2.
Composite deck so triggers screeding/leveling (Starken Drymix) + flooring finish (PPCH/Signature ABA SPC) specification. Concrete slab pour so triggers skim coat / plaster spec.
Frame type determines wall system: RC frame so infill AAC blocks (Starken). Steel frame so AAC panels or lightweight partitions. IBS columns/beams (G-Cast) so compatible AAC panel or block infill.

Mass concrete pours (Chin Hin Low Heat) so requires embedded conduit coordination with Engenious electrical. Deep foundations so triggers underground utility routing with piping (PPCH). Slab type affects floor box / trunking layout.

Concrete tile roofing (PPCH) so heavier truss / structural design vs. metal roofing (Ajiya Metal) so lighter steel truss (ARI/ARIT). Curtain wall glazing so requires steel/RC structural support brackets.
Ajiya Metal door/window frames so triggers internal finishing (architraves, skim coat around openings).

AAC Wall Panel (Starken) serves as both wall AND envelope element shared jurisdiction. External plaster (Starken Drymix 1-coat/2-coat) applied to envelope walls. Envelope insulation (PPCH Rockwool/Glasswool) integrates into walling cavity.

AAC block walls so REQUIRE AAC Thin Bed Adhesive + AAC Render OR Skim Coat (Starken). Brick walls (PPCH) so REQUIRE 3-coat plaster (Starken Drymix). RC walls so REQUIRE 2-coat skim coat (Starken Drymix). Wall type determines tile adhesive grade for wet areas.
External render (Starken Drymix) applied to AAC = envelope finishing.

Every wall selection triggers Agent 3. AAC so adhesive + render/skim coat. Brick so plaster + skim coat. All walls so paint, tiling, or other surface finish. Walling type determines waterproofing approach in wet areas (Starken Drymix Waterproofing Series).

Fire-rated AAC compartment walls so must maintain fire rating at service penetrations (Engenious fire protection). Acoustic walls so affects PA system design (Synergy M5). Wall thickness/material affects embedded conduit routing.
G-Cast L-Shaped Wall competes with / complements AAC in earth-retaining applications. Orchestrator should flag this overlap.

Transformer rooms, generator sets (Engenious) so structural slab/room design. Chiller plant (Vino/Engenious) so roof structural capacity for rooftop units. UPS rooms (Synergy M5) so floor loading for battery racks. Cable tray routing so structural penetration coordination.

Synergy M5 LED displays so affect interior feature wall design (Space Alliance). Stage engineering / lighting (Synergy M5) so ceiling void and structural coordination. CCTV/access control (Synergy M5/Engenious) so door hardware coordination (PPCH ironmongery in Agent 2). AV conferencing so furniture/joinery layout (Signature/Space Alliance).
Fire-rated wall penetrations must be sealed to maintain compartmentation. Conduit/pipe sleeves through AAC walls require compatible sealing.

Electrical manholes (G-Cast Precast Manhole/Substation Plinth) house Engenious HT/LT switchgear. External piping (PPCH) connects to Engenious sanitary/fire systems. Jacking pipes (G-Cast) used for trenchless utility crossings designed by MEP engineers.

Same concrete supplier (Chin Hin) serves building + infrastructure. Same piling contractor (PPCH) serves foundations + bridge abutments. Aggregates/earthworks (PPCH) shared between building platform and road base. Makna Setia heavy equipment services can support building structural works.
G-Cast L-Shaped Wall vs. Precast Sheet Piles vs. AAC orchestrator should present alternatives based on application (earth retaining vs. marine vs. boundary).

G-Cast electrical manholes/substation plinths so Engenious HT cable routing. G-Cast socket/rebated pipes + PPCH piping so Engenious water/sanitary systems. Site infrastructure (PPCH cabin, diesel, cables) supports MEP temporary power during construction.
Civil works (Makna Setia earthworks, G-Cast drainage) precede structural works. Orchestrator should sequence Agent 6 recommendations BEFORE Agent 1 in early phases.
